skill.name: create-pr
skill.description: Guides agents through creating and updating GitHub pull requests using Sentry-style review and description conventions.
skill.summary: Prepare concise, review-ready GitHub pull requests with Sentry-style descriptions and titles.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell command examples and hardcoded URLs in SKILL.md. The external commands are legitimate git and GitHub CLI workflow steps, but they can create or edit remote pull requests, so publication should include a clear operational warning. The weak cryptography detections appear to be false positives in prose and markdown examples; no cryptographic implementation or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: GitHub CLI Commands Can Modify Remote Repository State
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run gh pr create and gh api PATCH commands. This is expected for a pull request skill, but it can create draft pull requests and update PR titles or bodies on GitHub.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly shown and have remote write effects through authenticated gh. The intent matches the skill purpose, so this is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution Used In PR Body Examples
medium_findings.description: The PR creation and update examples use shell command substitution with heredoc content. The examples are static templates, but copied or generated body text should be reviewed before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution pattern is present in shell examples. The surrounding context is a legitimate gh workflow and does not show untrusted input being executed as a command.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs And Source Metadata Use Network References
low_findings.description: The skill contains GitHub and Sentry documentation URLs. These are ordinary references and do not by themselves send repository data to an unknown endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links and source metadata. No hidden callback, webhook, or data exfiltration behavior is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed locations are prose, markdown examples, or ordinary command text. No hash function, cipher choice, or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic code at the reported locations. The matches appear caused by substrings in normal English or markdown content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated External Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on git and gh commands that read repository state and interact with GitHub. Users should verify the generated PR title and body before running commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external commands are visible and central to the skill. They are appropriate for the task but can affect remote repository content when run with authenticated credentials.

content.value_statement: Writing clear pull request descriptions takes time and can miss reviewer context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured GitHub PR workflow.
actual_capabilities: Checks branch status and compares current changes against the default branch.
Guides review of commits and diffs before opening a pull request.
Provides a concise PR description structure focused on what changed and why.
Shows title conventions for feature, bug fix, and refactor pull requests.
Uses GitHub CLI commands to create draft PRs and update existing PR metadata.
Links users to Sentry code review and commit message guidance.
limitations: Requires an authenticated GitHub CLI installation for PR creation or updates.
Assumes the repository uses Sentry-style conventions, which may not match every team.
Does not run tests or verify code correctness before opening the PR.
Does not decide whether changes are ready for merge.

use_cases.title: Open A Draft Feature PR
use_cases.description: Prepare a focused draft pull request with a clear motivation and reviewer context.

use_cases.title: Document A Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Explain the failure, the fix, and the related issue in a compact PR body.

use_cases.title: Update Existing PR Metadata
use_cases.description: Revise a pull request title or body when review context changes after creation.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small committed change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill to inspect my current branch and prepare a draft pull request description.

prompt_templates.title: Write A Bug Fix PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused workflow for a fix linked to an issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill to write a bug fix PR body. Include the cause, the fix, and the issue reference.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare A Reviewer-Focused PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for changes that need careful review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill to review the branch diff and draft a PR body that highlights risk areas for reviewers.

prompt_templates.title: Update An Existing PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for revising PR metadata after new context appears.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill to update my existing PR title and description based on the latest branch changes.

output_examples.input: Create a draft PR for a feature branch that adds Slack thread replies for alerts.
output_examples.output: A concise PR description explaining the new alert thread behavior, why it reduces channel noise, and the related ticket reference.

output_examples.input: Prepare a PR body for a null response bug fix in a user API endpoint.
output_examples.output: A short bug fix summary that states the crash condition, the null handling change, and the issue being fixed.

output_examples.input: Update the description for an existing refactor PR after the scope changed.
output_examples.output: A revised PR body that explains the shared validation module, confirms behavior is unchanged, and notes the future work it enables.

best_practices: Review the git diff and commit list before creating the pull request.
Explain why the change exists, not only what files changed.
Create draft pull requests when the work needs early feedback.
anti_patterns: Do not bundle unrelated features or fixes into one pull request.
Do not replace useful reviewer context with a copied diff summary.
Do not run GitHub CLI write commands before reviewing the generated title and body.

faq.question: Does this skill create pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides GitHub CLI commands for creating draft pull requests. Users should review the generated title and body before execution.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow expects gh to be installed, authenticated, and available in the repository environment.

faq.question: Can it work outside Sentry repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but the recommended structure and title style follow Sentry conventions and may need adaptation.

faq.question: Does it include test plans?
faq.answer: No. The skill specifically avoids test plan sections and focuses on context, motivation, and review notes.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing PR?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows gh api PATCH commands for changing a pull request title or body.

faq.question: What should users verify before using it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm all changes are committed, inspect the diff, and review any command that writes to GitHub.
